summary: The optimal method involves administering Low Molecular Weight Heparins through a subcutaneous injection after surgery due to its proven efficacy across various studies supporting this approach over other methods including IV infusion given lower risks involved. Subcutaneously delivered LWMH offers simplicity, safety profile favorable compared to alternative delivery mechanisms making it superior choice clinically speaking under normal circumstances
